recentTweets.text:
Happy 4th of July, #CA13!

As America marks 250 years, today is a reminder that while we’ve never agreed on everything, we’ve always been strongest when we remember what we have in common.

Have a safe and meaningful Independence Day with your family and friends.

Team Gray joined local leaders and elected officials at the 6th annual Fresno Pride flag-raising ceremony to recognize and celebrate the contributions of the LGBTQ+ community in #CA13. https://t.co/QY2ur95WoV

The administration’s supplemental funding request includes a $10 billion investment for specialty crop growers, a critical priority for the Central Valley.

After a difficult year marked by trade uncertainty, rising costs, and weaker markets, this investment would provide the

Team Gray joined members of the Fresno Irrigation District for the ribbon-cutting ceremony celebrating the completion of the Carter-Bybee Basin Groundwater Recharge Basin.

This project will help strengthen groundwater supplies and improve long-term water reliability for https://t.co/6mXD33P8Rs

Army Sergeant Alan Frunz earned the Purple Heart in 1968 but never received the medal. Nearly six decades later, working with the U.S. Army, my office helped ensure Sergeant Frunz finally received the recognition he earned through his service and sacrifice. https://t.co/WPo8k95RZL
In Modesto, we brought together local labor leaders and organizers to discuss how we can work together to support folks across #CA13. https://t.co/IuSNbVH3d7

Antojitos Sinaloa brings authentic flavors and a welcoming community atmosphere to #CA13.

Already known for its food truck at Grub Hub Modesto, Team Gray was onsite to celebrate the grand opening of its new storefront on Carpenter Road. https://t.co/8k9OLDAKFt
Team Gray was in Modesto for the American Graffiti Festival & Car Show. A great event to support children and youth in our community. https://t.co/lVRiRwMCS2

Congratulations to the Modesto Symphony on 95 years of enriching the Central Valley’s music scene. Team Gray attended the ribbon-cutting ceremony to celebrate this incredible milestone. https://t.co/4cdar5kgbJ
The success of American agriculture depends on the success of California agriculture. https://t.co/QusW6BdI3f

When we don't have enough skilled workers, fewer homes get built in the Central Valley. The bipartisan Dignity Act will strengthen border security, fix our broken immigration system, and help ensure we have the workforce needed to build more homes and create opportunity in #CA13. https://t.co/XMXQ9gjvVC

This morning, Team Gray was in Newman at the ribbon-cutting ceremony for the Hills Ferry Road Bridge Replacement Project.

Replacing this outdated bridge will improve connectivity and help keep #CA13 communities on both sides of the San Joaquin River safe for years to come. https://t.co/azGoQhASpq

Spent some time in Stanislaus County at the annual Patterson Apricot Fiesta.

Patterson’s history is closely tied to California’s apricot industry. Generations of #CA13 growers helped make this community known as the Apricot Capital of the World. https://t.co/3d8QElK0ai
Team Gray was excited to be at Modern Woodmen Field for @modroadsters 12-1 opening night victory! https://t.co/INZMkYpDak

This Memorial Day, Team Gray was in Merced, Santa Nella, Los Banos, Madera, Newman, Lathrop, Patterson, Ceres, Modesto, Hughson, and more communities across #CA13 to honor and remember those who made the ultimate sacrifice in service to their country. https://t.co/mwO9LTN7el
This week, I had the chance to speak with members of the UCDC Presidential Faculty Fellows Program and discuss the work I’m doing on behalf of #CA13. https://t.co/x732ctdxQr

Last week, Team Gray was in Fresno County for the Mendota Community Center groundbreaking ceremony.

We helped secure $2 million dollars in community project funding to help make this a reality.

This new community center will be a great resource to the City of Mendota and the https://t.co/ADuCX5yHY0

In Congress, I often find myself in between the “talk more, spend more” crowd and the “cut everything and then change the subject” crowd.

Neither approach helps solve the water problems facing #CA13.

I asked @usbr how we can work together to ensure the Central Valley can https://t.co/bb8mp5v4W2

Happy 100 years, Valley Prescription & Compounding Pharmacy!

Team Gray was excited to help celebrate this milestone and recognize their dedication, diligence, and devotion to the people of #CA13. https://t.co/tL1nFqgg98
On Saturday, Team Gray joined members of the #CA13 community at the Lao New Year parade and celebration in Merced. https://t.co/tBQOu6TYHc

There’s a real gap between what Central Valley farmers are being told and what they’re seeing on the ground.

I pressed Secretary Burgum on the need for real action on water and agriculture priorities for #CA13. https://t.co/MsVhQN0CKW

Crystal Creamery has been a dairy institution for more than 125 years, helping provide sustainable dairy products to the Central Valley and beyond.

Team Gray was onsite in Modesto to celebrate the company’s recertification as a California Green Business. https://t.co/h2dhqgG9oD

Over the weekend, Team Gray attended Hmong American Day in downtown Merced. 

The event was a great opportunity to recognize and celebrate Hmong American heritage and culture here in the Valley. https://t.co/NvcwWorvJP

The Stanislaus County Peace Officers Association held a solemn memorial service in Hughson to honor those who lost their lives in service to their community. We will never forget your service and sacrifice. https://t.co/qmjzqL2QMU

Stopped by Fire Station #1 to check in with Chief Evans and members of the @cityofmodesto Fire Department.

We secured $850,000 in federal funding to support upgrades and improvements to the station, helping ensure firefighters can continue responding quickly and effectively to https://t.co/N7gCRgYNZU

Met with Chief Gillespie and members of the @ModestoPolice to see how more than $1 million in federal funding we secured for cutting-edge technology upgrades will help keep the Valley safe. https://t.co/CcelsHSzyG

In Stanislaus County for the grand opening and ribbon-cutting ceremony of Central Valley Craft House. Looking to support a family-owned small business? Stop by Central Valley Craft House in Patterson! https://t.co/E0vedtxQkj
The Ceres Street Faire is a great chance to get some local food and catch up with folks from the Valley. https://t.co/jju4oWROQB

Learn more.⬇️
https://t.co/Fq5xPFjRJj
Passing a bipartisan Farm Bill will keep agriculture strong in the Central Valley. https://t.co/V8ISwJtSEA
Last week the House passed three of my bipartisan bills. https://t.co/F2yjDNUPiN

Learn more.⬇️
https://t.co/ARj8bM0Zyj

A century-old redwood at the Merced courthouse reached the end of its life, but not its story.
https://t.co/9LmkqFUvsh
Great meeting with members of the California Agricultural Leadership Foundation to discuss the future of #CA13 agriculture. https://t.co/jQbtsOfRkr
My office won the Holiday Decorating Contest. We always bring the Valley to Congress. https://t.co/lJdG7mzdNT

As health care costs rise, we must act to protect Valley families from paying more.

That’s why I led a letter to Speaker Mike Johnson urging him to keep the House in session until we vote on ACA tax credits. https://t.co/avJZphiBMh
Read more about the letter: https://t.co/ifBdXJtWV7

I’m excited to report that my amendment to the Endangered Species Act, that models my bill the Valley Water Protection Act, advanced out of committee yesterday with bipartisan support. https://t.co/kxQm9KyObt
My amendment to the Endangered Species Act helps ensure Central Valley farmers get the reliable water deliveries they need to keep producing. https://t.co/eu0vKd9KJu

I am proud to have co-sponsored the bipartisan Whole Milk for Healthy Kids Act.

A bill that brings support to Valley dairies and better nutrition for kids across America. A true win for #CA13. https://t.co/MHbdzkK0OF
